disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began suddenly and all at once about 45 minutes ago (last time seen completely normal)
trigger_question_examples: When was he last completely normal?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on out of nowhere about 45 minutes ago — one moment he was fine having his coffee, the next he'd dropped the cup and this had all happened.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Unilateral drooping of one side of the face
trigger_question_examples: Is his face drooping on one side?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — the right side of his face has sagged, his mouth is pulling down on that side.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden one-sided arm and leg weakness
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His right arm and leg have gone weak and floppy — the arm just hangs there and he can't lift it, and he can't stand up.

trigger_question_examples: Is his speech slurred or making sense?
Can he get his words out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's trying to talk but it comes out slurred and doesn't make sense — he can't name things or get a sentence out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known atrial fibrillation with prescribed anticoagulant ceased about 3 months ago because of cost
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any heart conditions?
Is he on any regular medications — is he taking them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has an irregular heartbeat and was on a newer blood thinner for it, but he stopped about three months ago because it was too expensive.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient remains awake and appears to follow simple one-step requests but cannot speak
trigger_question_examples: Does he seem to understand you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's awake and looking at me and seems to understand simple things, but he just can't talk back.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No headache, no head injury, and no witnessed jerking/convulsion before this began
trigger_question_examples: Did he have a headache or hit his head?
Was there any shaking or fit before this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, he didn't complain of a headache and didn't hit his head, and there was no shaking or fit — it just came on suddenly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Difficulty swallowing implied by facial weakness and speech loss (aspiration risk)
trigger_question_examples: Has he tried to drink anything — did he cough or choke?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I haven't given him anything — I was worried he might choke because that side of his mouth isn't working.
